name: adv-discover
title: ADV Discover Command
purpose: Defines the rigor requirements for /adv-discover. The discover command gathers current-state evidence, investigates edge cases, scans for conflicts and related patterns, and produces structured findings for /adv-agree. It must extend prior research rather than rehash it.

requirements.title: Discovery Checklist Enforcement
requirements.body: /adv-discover output MUST contain a Discovery Checklist section listing each mandatory protocol step with PASS or SKIP and a reason. This ensures no protocol step is silently omitted.

scenarios.title: Checklist present in discovery output
given: A /adv-discover invocation completes successfully
scenarios.when: The discovery output is persisted via adv_change_update
then: The output contains a 'Discovery Checklist' section
The checklist lists at least 7 mandatory protocol steps
Each step has a PASS or SKIP status with a reason

requirements.title: Phase 1.5 Skill Discovery Enforcement
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST execute the skill discovery protocol from ADV_INSTRUCTIONS.md and emit a Skills Considered section. This prevents the agent from skipping domain-specific skill loading.

scenarios.title: Skill discovery executes and reports results
given: Trusted skill directories exist with SKILL.md files
then: The output contains a 'Skills Considered' section
The section lists examined skills with match assessment and action taken

scenarios.title: No skills available is reported explicitly
given: No SKILL.md files exist in trusted skill directories
then: The output reports 'Skills considered: none available'
The Skills Considered section is not omitted

requirements.title: Prior Research Extension
requirements.body: When prior research artifacts exist (temp/*.md, docs/*-prep.md including /adv-improve research packs, related archived changes), /adv-discover MUST cite them in an Extends section AND add at least one new finding beyond what they contained. This prevents discovery from rehashing prior work. When a cited pack contains Competitors & Alternatives or Emerging Patterns sections relevant to an open design question, /adv-discover MUST cite those sections in the LBP Check.

scenarios.title: Prior artifacts cited and extended
given: A prior research artifact exists in temp/, docs/, or a related archived change
then: The output includes an 'Extends' section citing the artifact
The output adds at least one new finding not present in the cited artifact

scenarios.title: No prior research is non-blocking
given: No prior research artifacts exist in any canonical location
then: The output reports 'No prior research found'
Discovery does not block or fail

scenarios.title: Relevant /adv-improve research pack sections are cited in LBP Check
given: A docs/*-prep.md research pack is cited in the Extends section
The pack contains Competitors & Alternatives or Emerging Patterns sections
At least one open design question names external tools or libraries as a realistic alternative
then: The LBP Check cites the specific pack sections relevant to the open question
The LBP Check does not silently ignore the pack content

requirements.title: Gated External-Solution Check
requirements.body: When the proposal's Discovery Agenda contains ecosystem unknowns, or an open design question lists external tools/libraries/services as a realistic option, /adv-discover MUST perform an External-Solution Check. It MUST first consult any cited docs/*-prep.md research pack; only when no relevant pack covers the question may it run fresh Kagi queries for competitors, alternatives, and emerging patterns. Purely internal changes (refactors, bug fixes, local doc/test fixes) may record 'No external alternatives apply' with rationale and skip the fresh search.

scenarios.title: Pack-covered ecosystem question cites pack instead of re-searching
given: The Discovery Agenda contains an ecosystem unknown
A docs/*-prep.md research pack already covers that ecosystem question
then: The LBP Check summarises findings from the pack and cites it
Fresh Kagi queries are not run for the same question

scenarios.title: Uncovered ecosystem question triggers fresh external search
given: The Discovery Agenda contains an ecosystem unknown
No cited docs/*-prep.md research pack covers that ecosystem question
then: Kagi queries for competitors/alternatives and emerging patterns are run
Findings are recorded in the LBP Check with source URLs
/adv-improve is recommended as a follow-up to persist the findings

scenarios.title: Purely internal change may skip with rationale
given: The change is a refactor, bug fix, or local doc/test update with no viable external alternative
then: The LBP Check records 'No external alternatives apply' with rationale
No external search is required

requirements.title: Conflict and Related-Work Scan
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST run adv_change_list (with includeArchived), adv_change_validate, and adv_agenda_list, and report results in a Conflict Scan section. This prevents the agent from proposing work that conflicts with existing or archived changes.

scenarios.title: All three mandatory tools are called
given: A change in the discovery phase
then: adv_change_list with includeArchived is called
adv_change_validate is called
adv_agenda_list is called
Results are reported in a 'Conflict Scan' section with explicit findings or 'no conflicts'

scenarios.title: Own-change validation warnings are excluded from conflict findings
given: adv_change_validate returns passed:false due to NO_TASKS or NO_DELTAS on the change being discovered
then: Own-change pre-prep warnings are not reported as external conflicts
The warnings are noted as expected pre-prep state

requirements.title: Edge Case Investigation
requirements.body: For each gap identified during discovery, /adv-discover MUST document at least 2 edge cases or failure modes, OR explicitly mark the gap as N/A: structural with rationale. This prevents shallow analysis that misses failure modes.

scenarios.title: Each gap has at least 2 edge cases
given: A gap identified during discovery that involves logic or behavior
then: The gap section contains at least 2 documented edge cases or failure modes

scenarios.title: Structural gaps allow N/A with rationale
given: A gap that is purely structural with no logic to test
then: The gap may be marked 'Edge cases: N/A — structural' with a rationale explaining why no edge cases apply

requirements.title: Design Question Depth
requirements.body: Each open design question in /adv-discover output MUST include trust model implications, blast radius, and alternatives considered. This prevents shallow one-liner questions that lack analysis depth.

scenarios.title: Each question has three required annotations
given: A discovery output with open design questions
then: Each question lists trust model implications (agent-only, user-only, or joint)
Each question lists blast radius (what breaks or changes if chosen wrong)
Each question lists alternatives considered (or 'none viable, single direction')

requirements.title: Draft Spec Delta Shapes
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST include draft spec deltas with concrete requirement IDs (rq-* format) and at least one Given/When/Then scenario per delta, OR explicitly state 'No spec deltas required' with rationale. This prevents vague 'spec deltas will be needed' placeholders.

scenarios.title: Drafted deltas include IDs and scenarios
given: A discovery that identifies spec changes are needed
then: Each draft delta has a concrete rq-* requirement ID
Each draft delta has at least one Given/When/Then scenario

scenarios.title: No-delta changes are declared explicitly
given: A discovery that determines no spec changes are required
then: The output states 'No spec deltas required' with a rationale
The spec deltas section is not silently omitted

requirements.title: P25 Related-Pattern Scan
requirements.body: /adv-discover MUST execute a related-pattern scan per rule P25 to detect similar bugs or gaps elsewhere in the codebase, and report results in a Related Pattern Scan section. This prevents fixing one instance while identical issues remain elsewhere.

scenarios.title: Related-pattern scan executes and reports
given: A change in the discovery phase
then: The output contains a 'Related Pattern Scan' section
The section lists similar patterns with file references or explicitly states 'no similar patterns found'

requirements.title: Methodology Skill Loading
requirements.body: /adv-discover SHOULD load an adv-discover-methodology skill using the Phase 0 pattern established by rationalizeCommandVsSkill, with inline fallback when the skill is unavailable. This aligns discovery with the command+skill LBP applied to review, harden, slop-scan, and tron.

scenarios.title: Skill loaded when available
given: The adv-discover-methodology skill exists in a trusted directory
then: skill('adv-discover-methodology') is invoked
Methodology guidance from the skill is applied during discovery

scenarios.title: Inline fallback when skill is unavailable
given: The adv-discover-methodology skill does not exist
then: A fallback notice is logged
The command continues with the embedded protocol from the command file
